title: Design System: Colormono AI Prompt Manager

title.purpose: App header title, modal titles. The largest text in the system.

label.purpose: Form field labels, badges, filter chips.

mono.purpose: Prompt bodies and other code-shaped content only.

shadows.name: sm
shadows.purpose: Default resting shadow on cards; barely perceptible.

shadows.name: md
shadows.purpose: Raised/active state, e.g. the active view-toggle button.

shadows.name: lg
shadows.purpose: Reserved for the modal, the one true overlay in the system.

motion.name: transition-fast
motion.purpose: Icon-button and small control hover/active feedback.

motion.name: transition-base
motion.purpose: Theme switch, larger surface color transitions.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Default call-to-action button. The only place color signals "act on this".
components.css: .ds-btn { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; border-radius: 8px; border: 1px solid transparent; font-weight: 500; padding: 8px 16px; font-size: 1rem; transition: background-color 120ms ease, border-color 120ms ease, color 120ms ease; } .ds-btn-primary { background-color: #5b63f5; color: #ffffff; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background-color: #4348dc; } .ds-btn-primary:active { background-color: #3336ab; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #5b63f5; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Non-primary action; sits beside Primary without competing.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { background-color: #ffffff; border-color: #c3c9d4; color: #16181f; } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { background-color: #f8f9fb; } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #5b63f5; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Danger Button
components.css: .ds-btn-danger { background-color: #d1373f; color: #ffffff; } .ds-btn-danger:hover { opacity: 0.9; } .ds-btn-danger:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #5b63f5; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Labeled text field with help/error message slot.
components.css: .ds-field { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 4px; } .ds-field-label { font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; color: #16181f; } .ds-field-control { padding: 8px 12px; border-radius: 8px; border: 1px solid #c3c9d4; background-color: #ffffff; color: #16181f; font-size: 1rem; transition: border-color 120ms ease; } .ds-field-control::placeholder { color: #545c6d; } .ds-field-control:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #5b63f5; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Card
components.description: Flat-leaning container: hairline border does the separating, shadow is a whisper.
components.css: .ds-card { display: flex; flex-direction: column; background-color: #ffffff; border: 1px solid #dde1e8; border-radius: 12px; box-shadow: 0 1px 2px rgba(12, 13, 17, 0.06); } .ds-card-body { padding: 16px; }

components.name: Brand Badge
components.description: Pill tag for tags/categories; status color only, never decoration.
components.css: .ds-badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; padding: 4px 12px; border-radius: 999px; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1.2; } .ds-badge-brand { background-color: #e4e7ff; color: #5b63f5; }

narrative.overview: This is a control surface, not a storefront — a precise instrument built for exactly one operator, used mid-workflow to find, copy, and get back to work in seconds. Every visual decision serves retrieval speed and quiet confidence: the interface recedes so the cataloged content (a prompt's actual text, a tool's description) stays the visual focus, and the one saturated color in the system — Electric Indigo — exists to mean a single thing: act on this. The system rejects the SaaS-dashboard reflex of card-grid monotony, gradient text, hero metrics, and decorative eyebrows named directly in PRODUCT.md's anti-references. Nothing here needs to sell itself; it already has its one user. Density and precision read as respect for that user's time, not as sterility — buttons and controls stay tactile and confident (clear hover/active states, real color shifts) rather than flat and inert. Light and dark are both first-class: the same semantic role names resolve to different neutral/brand steps per theme, so no component ever branches on theme directly.
keyCharacteristics: One accent color (Electric Indigo), used sparingly and only for actionable/interactive emphasis
Flat-leaning surfaces with hairline borders; shadows present but whispered, never the primary depth cue
System font stack — no custom display type, because there is no display moment to serve
Tight, disciplined spacing scale (4px base) and a small, consistent radius set
Fast, unshowy motion (120–200ms ease) on state changes only

rules.name: The One Signal Rule
rules.body: Electric Indigo appears only where the user can act: buttons, links, active toggles, focus rings, checkboxes. It never appears as a background wash, a gradient stop, or plain decoration.

rules.name: The Whisper Shadow Rule
rules.body: Depth comes from hairline borders first, shadow second. A component that needs a heavy shadow to read as "raised" should get a border instead.

rules.name: The No-Hero Rule
rules.body: No font size in this system exceeds 1.25rem (Title). If a screen seems to need something bigger, the answer is better information architecture, not a bigger headline.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest. A shadow above sm only appears in direct response to state (raised/active) or true overlay (modal); it is never used to make an otherwise-static element look "designed".

dos: Do keep Electric Indigo (#5b63f5 / #a5adfb dark) to actionable elements only: buttons, links, focus rings, active states, checkboxes.
Do lead with a 1px Hairline border for surface separation; add shadow only for raised/active state or the modal overlay.
Do use the system font stack at the existing size steps (0.75rem–1.25rem); resist introducing a display face or a size above 1.25rem.
Do give every interactive primitive a clear, immediate hover/active color shift — the tactile-and-confident standard.
Do compose new feature UI from the existing primitives (Button, Input, Select, Checkbox, Card, Badge, Modal, Textarea) rather than one-off styling.
donts: Don't use card-grid monotony, gradient text, hero-metric layouts, or tiny uppercase eyebrow labels — named anti-references from PRODUCT.md.
Don't reach for glassmorphism or decorative blur; this system has none.
Don't let a status color (green/amber/red) appear anywhere except status/state contexts — they are not decorative accents.
Don't introduce a heavy shadow (lg) outside the modal; it's reserved for the one true overlay.
Don't use border-left/border-right as a colored accent stripe on cards or list rows.
Don't add motion beyond the existing 120–200ms ease state-change transitions; there is no choreographed/scroll-driven motion in this system.
